name: Isaiah Likely
headline: Free from Andrews, Likely inherits a genuine starter's role
body:
For four years Likely was a part-time piece behind Mark Andrews, and the underlying numbers still hint at what's been bottled up. In 2024 he posted a 0.478 EPA per play with a 63.8% success rate and turned 58 targets into six scores; even a banged-up, 12-game 2025 yielded a 0.15 EPA mark, 75% catch rate and a tidy 1.29 yards per route. He's never crossed 60 targets in a season. That ceiling is gone now.

The Giants paid $40 million over three years to make him the centerpiece tight end, and outside Malik Nabers (rehabbing an ACL) the pass-catching depth is thin. The path to a career-high target share is obvious, even if a run-leaning scheme caps the absolute volume. Projections near 66 catches and 790 yards make him a back-end TE1 worth a late-round shot around pick 130. The talent has always been there; finally so is the opportunity.
